Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer in the Buddy Clean application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the customer. (Firebase Auth UID)

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a customer.

serviceIds.description: References to Services. (Relationship: Order N:N Service)

orderDate.description: The date the order was placed.

serviceDate.description: The date the service is scheduled for.

totalAmount.description: The total amount for the order.

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., Razorpay, Cash on Delivery).

status.description: The status of the order (e.g., pending, confirmed, completed, cancelled).

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a cleaning service offered by Buddy Clean.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., Home Cleaning, Car Wash).

imageUrl.description: URL of an image representing the service.

variants.description: Variants of the service (e.g., 1BHK, 2BHK for Home Cleaning, 5-seater, 7-seater for Car Wash).

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a message received from the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact message.

name.description: The name of the person sending the message.

email.description: The email address of the person sending the message.

sentDate.description: The date the message was sent.

AdminRole.title: AdminRole
AdminRole.description: Represents an administrator role, granting access to the admin panel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the admin role (Firebase Auth UID).

isAdmin.description: Indicates whether the user has admin privileges.

definition.description: Stores customer profile information. The customerId is the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

definition.description: A subcollection to store orders for each customer. Includes denormalized 'customerId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

definition.description: Stores details for each cleaning service.
Stores messages from the contact form. Access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: contactId
params.description: Unique identifier for the contact message.

definition.description: Identifies administrators by their Firebase Auth UID. Existence of a document grants admin privileges.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization, avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. Structural Segregation is used to maintain a homogeneous security posture within each collection. Access Modeling employs path-based ownership for user-owned data and membership maps for collaborative data.

Specifically:

*   `/customers/{customerId}`: Stores customer data, with `customerId` matching the Firebase Auth UID. This ensures that only the authenticated user can access their data (path-based ownership).
*   `/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}`: A subcollection storing orders for each customer. The `customerId` is denormalized into each order document to maintain Authorization Independence. This allows security rules to validate that the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) matches the `customerId` in the order document without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent customer document. This structure adheres to the principle of Authorization Independence. This facilitates secure `list` operations because rules can directly filter based on the denormalized `customerId`.
*   `/services/{serviceId}`: Stores service details. These are globally accessible but can be secured based on admin roles if needed.
*   `/contacts/{contactId}`: Stores contact form submissions. Access is restricted to administrators.
*   `/roles_admin/{uid}`: This collection uses Existence over Content for global role management. The existence of a document with the user's UID grants admin privileges. This is checked in the security rules to determine if a user can access the admin panel or perform administrative functions. It supports QAPs because the rule simply checks for the existence of a document and not its content.

This design achieves Authorization Independence by denormalizing the customerId into the order subcollection, enabling atomic operations and simplifying security rules. Structural Segregation is applied by separating user data, service data, contact messages, and admin roles into distinct collections with clearly defined security requirements. The use of path-based ownership and existence checks for admin roles simplifies access control and supports efficient querying.
